comments.author: @MarkCovington-sn8bj
comments.text: Interesting video, even though you got some things wrong or completely omitted. I lived and worked there for 27 years. Most of the people I worked with are gone. Deaf from strange illnesses mostly cancers. Testing is still going on there. Some of the nicest friends I have ever had were Marshallese. The entire area is a nuclear wasteland. Atomic weapons should be banned. We should be ashamed of how we treated those people. All for national security. What a disgrace!

replies.author: @RoamPlanet-4K
replies.text: Watching the contrast between the overcrowded Ebeye island and the multi-million dollar US missile base on Kwajalein right across the lagoon is heartbreaking. One side struggles for every square meter of land to survive, while the other boasts state-of-the-art technology. Is the COFA funding truly an economic 'lifeline' that sustains this nation, or is it just a cheap price paid by the US to legalize turning this place into a military backyard while dodging full accountability for past nuclear disasters? What do you all think about the fairness here?

replies.author: @NovaTerrain-4K
replies.text: Watching the part about the Runit Dome and climate change really highlights a gross global injustice. The Marshallese people didn't cause greenhouse gas emissions, nor did they create nuclear weapons, yet they suffer the worst twin consequences. The US established a compensation fund, but is it ever truly enough for the genetic damage (congenital disorders, cancers) and the imminent threat of losing their entire nation to rising seas? Or is it just a way to pay off and wash their hands of legal obligations after handing over a leaking nuclear tomb?
